mission.title: Install and Configure zot
mission.description: zot is a CNCF production-ready, vendor-neutral OCI-native container image registry. It provides a minimal, performant registry that supports OCI image and artifact specs with built-in search, deduplication, and signing.

steps.title: Deploy zot to Kubernetes
steps.description:
Deploy a minimal zot registry using kubectl.

```bash
kubectl create namespace zot
kubectl apply -n zot -f https://raw.githubusercontent.com/project-zot/zot/main/examples/k8s/deployment.yaml
```

steps.title: Expose zot service
steps.description:
Create a service to expose the zot registry.

```bash
kubectl expose deployment zot -n zot --port=5000 --target-port=5000 --type=ClusterIP
```

steps.title: Verify zot is running
steps.description:
Check the zot pod is running.

```bash
kubectl get pods -n zot
```

> You should see the zot pod in Running state.

steps.title: Test the registry
steps.description:
Port-forward and test the zot registry API.

```bash
kubectl port-forward svc/zot -n zot 5000:5000 &
curl http://localhost:5000/v2/_catalog
```

uninstall.title: Uninstall zot
uninstall.description:
Remove zot from your cluster.

```bash
kubectl delete namespace zot
```
